edgeCases: Verdict signal: orchestration ValidateEvidence only proves schema; APPLY vs ESCALATE is read from handoffTo (complete=APPLY, user=ESCALATE) post-validation — qa-senior must assert both branches.
Idempotent marker: WritePending uses truncating os.WriteFile, so re-running merge (text-conflict then validate-fail) rewrites reason in place, never appends — assert exactly one marker after rerun.
Dirty-tree precedence: ResolveMerge runs isDirty BEFORE the validator, so a dirty main tree blocks finalize even with valid APPLY evidence and exits non-zero.

Missing vs invalid evidence both refuse to finalize but surface different orchestration validator errors ('missing evidence json' vs 'mismatched/incomplete') — qa-senior should cover both error strings reach the user.
Hook globs ALL .workflow/*-merge-pending.json (out-of-band, not tied to an active workflow); goes silent the moment stewardEvidenceValidator returns nil error OR the marker is gone.
ESCALATE path prints RenderMergeEscalated + the steward report and *-merge-steward.diff sibling to stderr; worktree AND marker are kept; exit non-zero (no silent resolution).
Regression guard: clean merge (no conflict, validate passes) writes no marker, prints no directive, removes the worktree, exits 0 — unchanged from parent feature.
LoadPending returns (nil,nil) for a missing marker but a typed error for corrupt JSON, so 'no pending merge' and 'broken marker' never conflate.

G2 adapter: StewardEvidenceValidator func type in internal/worktree/finalize.go; the orchestration wrapper lives only in cmd/centinela — verified via go list -deps that internal/worktree has no orchestration dependency.
